title: Intermediaries, Hop-by-Hop Fields, Forwarding, and Transformation · Systems Transfer
summary: An independent protocol team receives different fragmentation, peer, trust, timing, and failure constraints and transfers intermediaries, hop-by-hop fields, forwarding, and transformation into a new intermediary forwarding and transformation contract. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of intermediary forwarding and transformation contract with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debu…
objectives: Remove Connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop.
Model downstream and upstream connections, framing, versions, deadlines, addresses, TLS, and failures as independent protocol hops.
Update framing, validators, digests, content codings, warnings, and signatures when an intermediary transforms content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Hop By Hop Removal
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab protocol team handles case 1944 in a archival client parsing incomplete close-delimited responses. Build deterministic Go evidence to strip dynamically nominated hop fields and repair metadata after transformations; preserve this operating constraint: diagnostics must omit credentials and attacker-controlled metric labels; then append a second pipelined message and prove the first parser leaves the exact residual bytes. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Hop By Hop Removal.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for remove connection and every nominated hop-by-hop field before forwarding, then establish fields for the new hop. For Commit to the mission model · Lab Hop By Hop Removal, keep Hop By Hop Removal connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Intermediary Failure Ownership
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Intermediary Failure Ownership: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A lab protocol team handles case 1953 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to strip dynamically nominated hop fields and repair metadata after transformations; preserve this operating constraint: no malformed framing may be normalized and forwarded to another recipient; then inject Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length and close before any downstream forwarding. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Assign parse, policy, connect, timeout, upstream, transform, and downstream-write failures to the correct hop and safe response boundary.
